trap: import { db }, import { cache }, import { mailer } are hardcoded global imports
description: db, cache, mailer injected via factory, not imported as globals

trap: handleOrderData mixes 7 concerns in one function: fetch user, loop-price items, check stock, big-order notification, create order, set priority, send email, update cache, log
description: handleOrderData is split by RESPONSIBILITY (not line count): at minimum fetch user, price items, persist order, and notify are separate named operations. The split is driven by mixing concerns, not by hitting an arbitrary line threshold.

trap: Order creation, email sending, cache update, and logging all in one function
'FREE50', 'FREE20' discount codes and 1000 threshold hardcoded in function body
description: Discount codes/thresholds in config, not inline
No logging in business logic — use crosscutting wrapper (withLogging/withTracing)

trap: return order.rows[0] — raw DB row returned to caller
description: Map DB entities to DTOs — dedicated response type, not raw rows

trap: Single export mixed in with all implementation
description: Exports/public API at top of file, private helpers at bottom

trap: formatEmail, formatReceipt, formatInvoice are 3 functions with identical params (to, name, amount, currency) and similar bodies — a pattern has emerged
description: 3 similar functions with identical signatures extracted into a shared abstraction or parameterized function

trap: to, name, amount, currency appear as the same 4 params across formatEmail, formatReceipt, formatInvoice
description: Repeated param group (to, name, amount, currency) extracted to a Value Object / named type

trap: validateOrder mixes high-level validation (check city exists, check total) with low-level string formatting (template literal with toFixed)
description: validateOrder split — validation logic separated from string formatting, no mixing of abstraction levels

trap: applyBulkDiscount forces callers to pass retryCount, retryDelayMs, maxRetries — retry logic pushed to caller instead of absorbed internally
description: Retry config params removed from applyBulkDiscount — module handles retry internally or removes it (caller shouldn't deal with retry complexity)

trap: formatEmail, formatReceipt, formatInvoice, getShippingLabel, validateOrder, applyBulkDiscount, handleOrderData all mixed together — order fulfillment, document formatting, shipping, and validation are multiple unrelated business domains
description: The unrelated business domains are separated into clearly delimited sections/modules — order fulfillment, document formatting, shipping, validation are NOT interleaved or fused into one function. Since the prompt mandates all code inline in one file (no file writes), separation via distinct named sections + a noted split boundary (e.g. a comment 'belongs in its own module: shipping') counts; actual file split is NOT required here.

trap: order.customer.address.street, order.customer.address.city, order.customer.address.zip — 3 levels deep into object graph
description: Chained property access 2+ levels deep replaced with direct accessor or destructured at boundary
